cta.title: Ready to start your healing journey?
cta.description: Schedule your first consultation to discuss your health goals and learn how Traditional Chinese Medicine can help you return to your best state.

hero.title: Meet Dr. Huang
hero.subtitle: Committed to your health and wellness through Traditional Chinese Medicine
hero.description: We support your recovery with time-tested wisdom and personalized care.

clinic.title: About Our Clinic

values.title: Compassionate Care
values.description: We treat every patient with respect, compassion, and genuine care for their well-being.

values.title: Clinical Excellence
values.description: We maintain high standards of practice, using quality herbal formulas and sterile techniques.

values.title: Patient-Centered Approach
values.description: Your needs, concerns, and goals are at the center of everything we do.

values.title: Natural Healing
values.description: We believe in supporting the body's natural ability to heal.

description: Dr. Huang Clinic was founded in 2010 with a simple mission: to provide accessible, high-quality Traditional Chinese Medicine care for the Middletown community.
What began as a small clinic has grown into a trusted wellness center that has helped over 1,000 patients achieve better health naturally.
clinic.environment: Our clinic offers a calm and welcoming environment designed to support relaxation and recovery. From the moment you arrive, you feel the difference: peaceful tones, comfortable treatment rooms, and a warm, patient-friendly atmosphere.

members.name: Prof. Wang Weidong
members.title: Chief Physician
members.description: Chair of the TCM Psychology Committee, Executive Vice Chair of the World Federation of Chinese Medicine Sleep Medicine Committee, and Vice President of the China Sleep Research Society. Key discipline leader in TCM psychology under the National Administration of Traditional Chinese Medicine. Former Vice President of Guang'anmen Hospital. Specializes in insomnia, anxiety, depression, OCD, and other psychological disorders. I studied under him in the Guang'anmen Hospital psychology and sleep clinic from 2010 to 2013.

members.name: Prof. Liu Yanjiao
members.title: MD, Chief Physician
members.description: Clinical Distinguished Expert at Beijing University of Chinese Medicine and Honorary President of the World Federation of Chinese Medicine Sleep Medicine Committee. Focuses on Chinese sleep medicine theory and clinical practice. Specializes in sleep disorders (insomnia, narcolepsy, snoring, sleep apnea), allergic conditions (asthma, hay fever, dermatitis), and neuroendocrine disorders.

members.name: Prof. Hua Baojin
members.title: Chief Physician, MD
members.description: Former Vice President of Guang'anmen Hospital and former Executive Deputy Director of the Chinese Academy of Chinese Medical Sciences Oncology Institute. National lead specialist for clinical TCM oncology. Research focuses on preventing postoperative recurrence/metastasis and applying classical formulas in cancer treatment.

members.name: Prof. Zhang Ruxin
members.title: Chief Physician, Professor
members.description: Graduated from Beijing School of Chinese Medicine in 1954. Expert in meridian qi activation and conduction techniques. Former committee member of the American TCM Evaluation Board and professor at the American International Acupuncture Institute. Known for strong results in facial spasm, trigeminal neuralgia, frozen shoulder, asthma, and sciatica.

members.name: Prof. Guo Rongjuan
members.title: Chief Physician, Doctoral Supervisor
members.description: President of the World Federation of Chinese Medicine Psychosomatic Medicine Committee and Chair of the Chinese Association of Traditional Chinese Medicine Psychosomatic Branch. Leader of the Wang Yongyan heritage station in Beijing's TCM inheritance program, with focus on psychosomatic medicine and integrated stroke prevention/treatment.

members.name: Prof. Tian Conghuo
members.title: Chief Physician, National Master Physician
members.description: Former Director of Acupuncture at Guang'anmen Hospital and national representative inheritor of the acupuncture intangible cultural heritage program. Mentor in the national 500 renowned TCM physician apprenticeship program. Family-lineage physician; graduated from China Medical University in 1951. Highly respected in acupuncture academic inheritance.

members.name: Prof. Sang Xisheng
members.title: Professor, Postdoctoral Scholar
members.description: Director of Basic TCM Theory Teaching and Research at Heilongjiang University of Chinese Medicine. Advocates a disease-centered TCM framework and has unique insights in internal medicine, gynecology, pediatrics, dermatology, and pain disorders. Co-developed the 'Yi-Needle' technique with colleagues, showing notable clinical outcomes. Standing Committee member of the CSCO expert committee.

members.name: Prof. Lin Xiaofeng
members.title: Chief Physician
members.description: Chief Physician at the First Affiliated Hospital of Heilongjiang University of Chinese Medicine. Specializes in cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases, including coronary heart disease and myocarditis, as well as difficult cases. Research focuses on zang-fu syndrome mechanisms. I studied under him in outpatient practice from 2009 to 2010.

members.name: Prof. Mao Dapeng
members.title: Chief Physician
members.description: Senior Beijing community TCM physician from a family lineage, inheriting the Yishui school and earth-tonifying doctrine. Over 40 years of clinical practice; developed the 'Qi-Boosting and Collateral-Filling' method for cardio-cerebrovascular disorders. Specializes in diabetes, cardiovascular/cerebrovascular disease, nephrotic syndrome, and geriatric insomnia.

members.name: Dr. Xiao Dehua
members.title: Needle-Knife Specialist
members.description: Early practitioner of needle-knife medicine, trained under its inventor Prof. Zhu Hanzhang. Serves as Vice Chair of the Needle-Knife Medicine Branch of the Chinese Association of Traditional Chinese Medicine. Focuses on education and promotion of needle-knife methods with strong pain-management outcomes. I trained with him in the first Beijing primary-care needle-knife program in 2021.

members.name: Dr. Wang Xiushen
members.title: Chief Physician
members.description: Third-generation inheritor of imperial court medicine lineage. Master of specialized techniques including multi-needle single-point method, penetrating traction needle, tongue acupuncture, and Five-Dragon needling. Over 50 years of clinical experience and inventor of many unique needling methods. Recognized internationally as a treasure in Chinese acupuncture.

members.name: Prof. Yang Zhixun
members.title: Renowned TCM Physician
members.description: Family-lineage TCM physician and Director of the Beijing Century Yang Therapy Institute. Specializes in treating multiple conditions simultaneously through external therapy and fluid purification principles. Dedicated to TCM education. I studied practical TCM catheter-based techniques under him from 2017 to 2018.

members.name: Prof. Zhou Wei
members.title: Chief Physician
members.description: Chief Physician at Beijing Huguosi TCM Hospital. Trained in acupuncture under Wang Juyi and internal medicine under Zhou Yaoting, Hao Wanshan, and other masters. Specializes in meridian medicine and Five Movements and Six Qi theory. I attended his elite meridian diagnosis training in 2018.

profile.bio:
Master's degree holder in Traditional Chinese Medicine. Completed undergraduate studies at Heilongjiang University of Chinese Medicine and master's studies at the China Academy of Chinese Medical Sciences. Selected into a senior-mentor academic inheritance program and trained under multiple nationally recognized TCM masters.

With more than a decade of clinical practice, Dr. Huang integrates clinical care and research, and specializes in acupuncture, herbal medicine, and integrative TCM approaches for psychological and sleep disorders, internal medicine, surgery, gynecology, pediatrics, difficult chronic conditions, and various pain syndromes.

> True healing comes from addressing the root cause, not simply suppressing symptoms. Every patient is unique, and treatment must reflect that individuality.

**Dr. Huang, Licensed Acupuncturist, Master of TCM**
profile.name: Dr. Huang, Licensed Acupuncturist, Master of TCM
profile.title: Licensed Acupuncturist and Licensed Traditional Chinese Medicine Practitioner in the United States

philosophy.title: Acupuncture Treatment Process

principles.title: TCM Diagnosis and Constitution Assessment
principles.description: Dr. Huang creates a treatment plan based on your condition and explains the general treatment areas for acupuncture.

principles.title: Needle Insertion
principles.description: Dr. Huang uses sterile disposable needles (or strictly sterilized tools) and inserts them at specific depths into selected acupoints.

principles.title: Needle Manipulation
principles.description: Dr. Huang gently moves or rotates the needles, which may create sensations such as soreness, tingling, heaviness, or distention.

principles.title: Needle Removal
principles.description: After approximately 20 minutes of retention, Dr. Huang removes the needles. Most patients feel little to no discomfort. A mild residual ache or fullness may occur in some people and is a normal acupoint response that usually fades quickly.

philosophy.introduction: Dr. Huang's treatment approach is rooted in the core principles of Traditional Chinese Medicine while integrating modern clinical understanding.

items: Classical herbal medicine seminars with Dr. Heiner Fruehauf
Annual participation in national TCM conferences
Regular study groups with local TCM practitioners
30+ hours of continuing education annually (requirement: 30 hours every 3 years)
continuingEducation.title: Continuing Education and Training
continuingEducation.description: Dr. Huang actively participates in the TCM community and regularly pursues advanced training:
